APSC100.detailed_summary: engineering design process; decision-making frameworks; sustainability considerations; technical communication; stakeholder analysis; design specifications; weighted decision matrices; ethical theories (utilitarianism, duty-based, rights-based); 7 Cs of effective communication
APSC100.high_level_modules: Engineering Design Process; Ethical Decision Making; Technical Communication; Sustainability Principles

APSC101.detailed_summary: engineering risk management (identification, analysis, mitigation strategies); hierarchy of controls; engineering drawings; team dynamics; diversity; feedback; engineering proposal process; sustainability; life cycle assessment; appropriate technology
APSC101.high_level_modules: Engineering Risk Management; Team Dynamics; Engineering Proposals; Sustainability Assessment

APSC160.official_name: Introduction to Computation in Engineering Design
APSC160.detailed_summary: C programming concepts; binary/hexadecimal conversions; control flow (loops, conditionals); functions; file I/O; arrays; string manipulation; Arduino programming; digital/analog I/O; serial communication; random number generation
APSC160.high_level_modules: C Programming Fundamentals; Control Flow; Arduino Programming

APSC278.detailed_summary: material properties; chemical bonding; crystal structures (BCC, FCC, HCP); mechanical behaviors (elastic/plastic deformation, stress-strain relationships, toughness); material failure mechanisms (fracture, fatigue, ductile-brittle transition); strengthening materials; risk assessment in engineering systems
APSC278.high_level_modules: Material Properties; Crystal Structures; Mechanical Behavior; Material Failure; Strengthening Mechanisms

CPEN221.detailed_summary: concurrency; multi-threading; parallel programming; synchronization primitives (Fork-Join parallelism); software design principles (mutability, immutability, Abstract Data Types (ADTs), representation invariants, Liskov Substitution Principle); computer systems; compilers; interpreters; regular expressions
CPEN221.high_level_modules: Software Design Principles; Computer Systems; Object-Oriented Programming; Concurrency and Parallelism

CPEN312.detailed_summary: 8051 microcontroller architecture; instruction sets; addressing modes; memory organization; interrupts; timers; assembly language programming; digital logic fundamentals; Boolean algebra (De Morgan's theorems); K-maps; combinational circuits (adders, encoders, multiplexers); sequential circuits (flip-flops, counters, shift registers)
CPEN312.high_level_modules: Digital Logic Fundamentals; Boolean Algebra;  Microcontroller Architecture; Assembly Programming;

ELEC204.detailed_summary: fundamental circuit concepts; voltage; power; Ohm's Law; Kirchhoff's Laws (KCL, KVL); resistor connections; network transformations; circuit analysis methods (Nodal analysis, Mesh analysis, Source Transformation, Thévenin's theorem, Norton's theorem); first and second-order RC/RLC circuits; transient and steady-state responses; unit step and impulse functions
ELEC204.high_level_modules: Fundamental Circuit Concepts; Circuit Analysis Methods; First- and Second-Order Circuits

ELEC221.detailed_summary: continuous and discrete-time signals; Fourier series and transforms (DTFT, CTFT); Linear Time-Invariant (LTI) systems analysis (convolution, impulse response, frequency response); signal energy/power; complex exponentials; sampling; modulation; Laplace Transform (Region of Convergence (ROC))
ELEC221.high_level_modules: Fourier Analysis; LTI System Analysis; Sampling and Modulation; Laplace Transform

ELEC302.detailed_summary: Laplace Transforms for circuit analysis; Nodal and Loop analysis; voltage regulators (linear, switching, buck converters); semiconductor devices (diodes (pn junction, Zener)); Bipolar Junction Transistors (BJTs) (small-signal models, configurations); MOS transistors (NMOS, PMOS) (structures, operating regions, amplifier configurations)
ELEC302.high_level_modules: Voltage Regulators; Semiconductor Diodes; BJT Transistors; MOS Transistors

ELEC341.detailed_summary: Laplace Transforms for LTI system analysis; transfer functions; poles; zeros; stability criteria; step responses for first and second-order systems; block diagram manipulation; modeling mechanical systems with electrical analogies; PID control (components, design process, delays, filters, non-linearities)
ELEC341.high_level_modules: LTI System Analysis; System Stability; Block Diagram Modeling; PID Control

ELEC431.detailed_summary: communication system components; probability and random processes; modulation techniques (PAM, PSK); error correction coding (Hamming codes, CRC, convolutional codes); signal space representation; power spectral density; Nyquist criterion; error probability calculations
ELEC431.high_level_modules: Probability and Random Processes; Modulation Techniques; Information Theory and Compression Encoding; Error Correction Coding

ENPH257.detailed_summary: fundamental laws of thermodynamics; ideal gas law; first and second laws of thermodynamics; internal energy; enthalpy; free energy; heat transfer mechanisms (conduction, convection, radiation); thermodynamic cycles (Carnot, Otto, Rankine)
ENPH257.high_level_modules: Fundamental Laws of Thermodynamics; Thermodynamic Cycles; Heat Transfer Mechanisms;

ENPH270.detailed_summary: rigid body kinematics (planar motion types, instantaneous center of rotation, relative velocity/acceleration); dynamics (Newton's laws, torque, moment of inertia, parallel axis theorem, work-energy principles for rigid bodies)
ENPH270.high_level_modules: Rigid Body Kinematics; Rigid Body Dynamics; Work-Energy Principles

MATH101.detailed_summary: definite and indefinite integrals; fundamental theorems of calculus; integration techniques (u-substitution, integration by parts, partial fractions); applications (areas, volumes of revolution, centroids); numerical integration methods (Riemann sums, Simpson's rule)
MATH101.high_level_modules: Integration Techniques; Applications of Integration; Sequences and Series

MATH152.detailed_summary: vector operations; dot and cross products; determinants; applications to lines and planes in 2D and 3D space; matrix algebra; inverses; linear transformations; eigenvalues; eigenvectors; solving differential equations; analyzing LCR circuits
MATH152.high_level_modules: Vector Algebra; Matrix Algebra; Linear Transformations; Eigenvalues and Eigenvectors

MATH217.detailed_summary: multivariable calculus concepts; vectors; partial derivatives; gradients; critical points; Lagrange multipliers; double and triple integrals in various coordinate systems; line integrals; surface integrals; fundamental theorems (Green's Theorem, Stokes' Theorem, Divergence Theorem); differential forms; de Rham cohomology
MATH217.high_level_modules: Multivariable Differentiation and Integration; Vector Calculus Theorems; Differential Forms

MATH255.detailed_summary: first-order ODEs (separable, linear, exact, autonomous); second-order linear ODEs (homogeneous, non-homogeneous, mechanical vibrations); solution methods (integrating factors, undetermined coefficients, variation of parameters, Laplace transforms); systems of ODEs analysis; phase plane sketching
MATH255.high_level_modules: First- and Second-Order ODEs; ODE Solution Methods; Systems of ODEs

MATH257.detailed_summary: solving ODEs using power series; PDEs (Heat equation, Wave equation, Laplace equation); solution techniques (separation of variables, Fourier series and transforms, eigenfunction expansions, D'Alembert's solution); Sturm-Liouville problems
MATH257.high_level_modules: Power Series for ODEs; Partial Differential Equations; PDE Solution Techniques; Sturm-Liouville Problems

MATH305.detailed_summary: complex numbers (Cartesian and polar forms); functions of complex variables; derivatives (Cauchy-Riemann equations, analytic functions); complex integration; Cauchy's Integral Theorem; Cauchy's Integral Formula; Taylor series; Laurent series; Residue Theorem (applications to real integrals, conformal mappings)
MATH305.high_level_modules: Complex Numbers; Complex Functions; Complex Integration; Series Expansions; Residue Theorem

MATH307.detailed_summary: solving systems of linear equations (Gaussian elimination, LU decomposition); vector spaces; norms; inner products; orthogonalization (Gram-Schmidt, QR decomposition); eigenvalues; eigenvectors; diagonalization; Singular Value Decomposition (SVD); Discrete Fourier Transform (DFT) (properties, applications)
MATH307.high_level_modules: Linear Equation Systems; Vector Spaces; Orthogonalization; Eigenvalue Problems; Matrix Decompositions

MATH318.detailed_summary: probability concepts (counting, conditional probability); discrete and continuous random variables (Bernoulli, Poisson, Normal, Gamma); statistical estimation; confidence intervals; hypothesis testing; Markov Chains (classification, stationary distributions, MCMC methods)
MATH318.high_level_modules: Probability Fundamentals; Random Variables; Statistical Inference; Markov Chains

MATH400.detailed_summary: methods for solving Partial Differential Equations (classification, Fourier Series, Eigenfunction Expansion, Laplace Transforms); Heat equation; Wave equation; Bessel's equation; Method of Characteristics for first-order PDEs (shock waves, expansion waves)
MATH400.high_level_modules: Eigenfunction Expansion; Wave, Heat and Bessel's Equations; Method of Characteristics

MECH260.detailed_summary: stress and strain concepts; Hooke's Law; Poisson's ratio; material properties; Mohr's Circle for stress analysis; yield criteria (Tresca, Von Mises); pressure vessel design; torsion; bending; shear force and bending moment diagrams; beam deflection methods; column buckling analysis (Euler's formula, secant formula)
MECH260.high_level_modules: Stress and Strain; Material Properties; Stress Analysis; Torsion and Bending; Column Buckling

MECH280.detailed_summary: fluid properties (density, viscosity, continuum hypothesis); fluid statics (pressure measurement, hydrostatic force); fluid kinematics; conservation laws (mass, momentum, energy); dimensional analysis; pipe flow; pumps; turbines (performance, design considerations)
MECH280.high_level_modules: Fluid Properties; Fluid Statics and Kinematics; Conservation Laws and Dimensionless Quantities; Fluid System Design

MECH325.detailed_summary: machine design concepts; flexible drives (belts, chains); gears (spur, helical, bevel); bearings (roller, ball); stress analysis; failure theories (static, fatigue); design of shafts, keys, couplings, and springs (selection, failure modes)
MECH325.high_level_modules: Machine Design Principles; Power Transmission Elements; Stress and Failure Analysis; Component Design

MECH360.detailed_summary: normal, shear, and bending stress and strain concepts; Hooke's Law; torsion; beam deflection (differential equations, superposition); critical buckling load formulas; Mohr's Circle for principal stresses; Castigliano's Theorem for strain energy
MECH360.high_level_modules: Torsion and Bending; Beam Deflection; Column Buckling; Mohr's Circle; Strain Energy

PHYS158.detailed_summary: electricity and magnetism concepts; magnetic forces on charges and currents (F=qvxB, F=ILxB); Biot-Savart Law; Ampere's Law; Gauss's Law for magnetism; DC circuits (resistors, capacitors, inductors); wave phenomena (interference, beats, standing waves); light behavior in different media; path difference calculations; phase shifts in interference patterns
PHYS158.high_level_modules: Electricity and Magnetism; AC/DC Circuits; Wave Phenomena and Interference

PHYS250.detailed_summary: Special Relativity; Lorentz transformations; time dilation; relativistic energy-momentum relation E² = P²c² + (mc²)²; Quantum Mechanics; photoelectric effect; Compton scattering; de Broglie wavelength; Heisenberg's Uncertainty Principle; Schrödinger equation for various potentials; Bohr's atomic model; quantum numbers (n, l, m)
PHYS250.high_level_modules: Special Relativity; Quantum Mechanics; Atomic Models; Wave-Particle Duality

PHYS301.detailed_summary: electrostatics; coordinate systems; charge distributions (volume, surface, line, point); Coulomb's Law; Gauss's Law (∮E.dA=Qenc/ε₀); electric potential (E=-∇V, V=kQ/r); capacitance (C=Q/ΔV) for various geometries; energy stored in capacitors; multipole expansion; polarization; dielectric materials
PHYS301.high_level_modules: Electrostatics; Electric Potential; Capacitance; Dielectric Materials; Multipole Expansion

PHYS304.detailed_summary: time-dependent and independent Schrödinger equations; wave function normalization; expectation values; Heisenberg uncertainty principle; solutions for infinite square wells, harmonic oscillators, hydrogen atom; angular momentum operators; commutators; spin; Pauli matrices
PHYS304.high_level_modules: Schrödinger Equation; Quantum Systems; Quantum Mechanics Formalism; Spin and Pauli Matrices

PHYS350.detailed_summary: Lagrangian and Hamiltonian mechanics; generalized coordinates; equations of motion; variational principles; Poisson brackets; small oscillations; normal modes; two-body problems (Kepler's laws); rigid body dynamics (Euler's equations)
PHYS350.high_level_modules: Lagrangian and Hamiltonian Mechanics; Variational Principles; Rigid Body Dynamics; Oscillations, Orbits and Rotation

PHYS401.detailed_summary: Maxwell's equations; conservation laws; Poynting vector; electromagnetic wave propagation; polarization; relativistic electromagnetism (Lorentz transformations, 4-vectors, field tensor)
PHYS401.high_level_modules: Maxwell's Equations; Electromagnetic Waves; Poynting Vector; Relativistic Electromagnetism

PHYS403.detailed_summary: entropy (S=klnΩ, S=-kΣp_ilnp_i); microcanonical, canonical, and grand canonical ensembles; partition functions (Z); thermodynamic potentials (F, Ω_G); Maxwell relations; response functions; quantum gases (Fermi-Dirac, Bose-Einstein, Planck distributions); phase transition models (Ising equation, Van der Waals equation)
PHYS403.high_level_modules: Statistical Ensembles; Partition Functions; Quantum Gases; Phase Transition Models

PHYS407.detailed_summary: general relativity; special relativity; Lorentz transformations; 4-vectors in spacetime; metric tensor; Christoffel symbols; geodesic equations; Riemann tensor; Ricci tensor; Einstein tensors; Schwarzschild solution for black holes
PHYS407.high_level_modules: Special Relativity; General Relativity; Spacetime Geometry; Orbits and Black Hole Solutions

PHYS408.detailed_summary: Maxwell's equations; wave optics phenomena (diffraction (Fraunhofer, Fresnel)); wave types (plane, spherical, Gaussian) and their equations; polarization states; Jones vectors; optical birefringence; wave plates; optical cavities and lasers (rate equations, gain, saturation, Q-switching)
PHYS408.high_level_modules: Wave Optics; Diffraction Phenomena; Polarization Optics; Optical Cavities; Laser Physics

CHEM154.detailed_summary: fundamental chemistry concepts; atomic structure; chemical bonding; periodic trends; molecular geometry (VSEPR); intermolecular forces; phases of matter; thermodynamics (energy, enthalpy, entropy); chemical kinetics (rate laws, mechanisms); electrochemistry (cell potentials, Nernst equation)
CHEM154.high_level_modules: Atomic Structure; Chemical Bonding; Thermodynamics; Chemical Kinetics; Electrochemistry

LATN101.detailed_summary: beginning Latin grammar; verb conjugations (present, perfect, imperative, subjunctive); irregular verbs ('sum', 'eo'); noun declensions (1st, 2nd); declension of demonstrative, personal, and interrogative pronouns
LATN101.high_level_modules: Latin Grammar; Verb Conjugations; Noun Declensions; Pronoun Declensions
